# ComponentAnimation

**Object**

Properties that all types of animations share.

## Declaration

```swift
object ComponentAnimation
```

## Discussion

Apply an animation to a component to add movement and interest to your article.



Apple News Format applies a `ComponentAnimation` just once each time someone views your article. (Compare that with component behaviors whose effects are persistent and happen every time someone sees the component in your article.)

A person can control some animations with an optional `userControllable` property.

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Component].

## Related Documentation

- **Adding Animations**: Use animations to affect how parts of your article come into view the first time they appear.

## Animations

- **About Component Animations**: Learn how to affect the way in which components come into view.
- **AppearAnimation**: An animation type whereby a component appears on the screen.
- **FadeInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component fades into view.
- **MoveInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component moves in from the side of the screen.
- **ScaleFadeAnimation**: The animation in which a component scales up and fades into view.

## Inherited By

- AppearAnimation
- FadeInAnimation
- MoveInAnimation
- ScaleFadeAnimation
